entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Students take folding to another level as they learn key techniques such as pleated surfaces, curved folding and crumpling. These techniques give them the tools to express their creativity and design their own models.

Sloyd: Level 7
Level 7 starts back at the beginning of sloyd with folding techniques. However, these folding techniques are of a higher thinking level, encouraging students to envision these techniques into building structures.  Paul Jackson has created videos to go along with some of the techniques, but students are encouraged to read the instructions carefully and figure these things out based on what they have learned in the previous models. While sloyd is beneficial for all students to envision geometrical forms in reality, Level 7 will be particularly interesting to the students who wish to go on to higher-level maths, engineering, design, architecture, and more.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
Grade 8 students who are new to sloyd should begin with Sloyd: Level 1 and then continue courses in sequence.

Prerequisite: Sloyd: Level 6

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Sloyd: Level 7
☐ View the "Sloyd Introduction Video" in the Quick Links below for an overview of materials and resources used in Sloyd.

☐ Allow time for loading and viewing videos if available. We recommend teachers preview tutorials and work a model before teaching it.

☐ Purchase supplies and introduce students to their Sloyd tools (discuss their care and safe use). Organize them so they are easily accessible.

☐ Students will need the book and A4 paper for these models. Some models will require the paper to be cut into a square or cut in half lengthwise before beginning.

entries.title: Term Prep & Teacher Tips
entries.content:
Sloyd: Level 7
A4 paper (This is different from traditional, American copy paper, though it looks very similar. The measurements will be completely off if you choose to use traditional copy paper.)

blocks.text: Lessons will go much more smoothly if you prepare thoroughly. As students gain experience, develop fine motor skills, and practice habits of precision they will need less support.
Watch video tutorials or make the model yourself. Gather supplies and materials needed.
Connect back to previous lesson and skills learned.
Read the instructions one step at a time. Model each step and make sure students complete it with precision before moving to the next step.
When a project is complete, students may repeat it as many times as they wish to give as gifts or create original patterns similar to the project.

lessons.title: 45m Sloyd: Level 7 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper (This is different from traditional, American copy paper, though it looks very similar. The measurements will be completely off if you choose to use traditional copy paper.)

Level 7 starts back at the beginning of sloyd with folding techniques. However, these folding techniques require higher-level thinking, encouraging students to envision them as ways to build structures.  Paul Jackson has created videos to go along with some of the techniques, but students are encouraged to read the instructions carefully and figure these things out based on what they have learned in the previous models. While sloyd is beneficial for all students to envision geometrical forms in reality, Level 7 will be particularly interesting to the students who wish to go on to higher-level maths, engineering, design, architecture, and more. 

PREP: Students will need the book and A4 paper for these models. Some models will require the paper to be cut into a square or cut in half lengthwise before beginning. 

➜ READ
Look through the diagrams on p.08 and read through the Introduction p.09-015. 

➜ PRACTICE
Watch the tutorial videos.
∞ Video Link: Basic Concepts
∞ Video Link: Pleating into Sixteenths
Work through the first basic concept of Linear Divisions: Sixteenths p.016-017.

lessons.title: 45m Sloyd: Level 7 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper

PREP: Gather and prepare supplies for this model.

➜ PRACTICE
Watch the tutorial video.
∞  Video Link: Pleating into Thirty-Seconds
Work through Linear Thirty-Seconds p.018-019.
If you have time remaining, work through any models you have previously completed multiple times.

Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper

PREP: Gather and prepare supplies for this model.

➜ READ
Read about grid divines on p.029

➜ PRACTICE
Work through Grid Divisions p.029-030.
These models are based on previously learned techniques. Look carefully at the diagrams to understand what you are to do. 
You will have three lesson times to work through these models. 
If you have time remaining, work through any models you have previously completed multiple times.

lessons.title: 45m Sloyd: Level 7 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper

PREP: Gather and prepare supplies for this model.

➜ READ
Read about Symmetrical Repeats p.031. For Term 2, you will be working through the four basic types of two-dimensional symmetry: translation, reflection, rotation, and glide reflection. You will have two to four weeks to work through each of these types. 

➜ PRACTICE
Watch video tutorials on symmetrical translations.
∞ Video Link: Symmetrical Motif 1
∞ Video Link: Symmetrical Motif 2
Work through the models on symmetrical translations p.031-034. You have two lessons to work through both of these models. If you finish with lesson time left, work through the models again to help further your understanding of these folds.

lessons.title: 45m Sloyd: Level 7 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper

PREP: Gather and prepare supplies for this model.

➜ PRACTICE
Watch video tutorials on symmetrical Glide Reflection. 
∞ Video Link: Glide Reflection
Work through the model on symmetrical glide reflection p.040-043. You have three lessons to work through all of the variation models. If you finish with lesson time left, work through the models again to help further your understanding of these folds. 

➜ DISCUSS
What can you build when you put multiples of these models together? 
Can you think of any buildings or structures you have seen that may be based on the glide reflection?

lessons.title: 45m Sloyd: Level 7 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper

PREP: Gather and prepare supplies for this model.

➜ READ
Read through Stretch & Skew on p.044.

➜ PRACTICE
Watch video tutorials on stretch & skew folds. Note that the video is not in English. The purpose is to see an example worked out. 
∞ Video Link: Stretch & Skew
Work through the models on stretch & skew p.044-049. You have five lessons to work through all of the variation models. If you finish with lesson time left, work through the models again to help further your understanding of these folds. 

➜ DISCUSS
What can you build when you put multiples of these models together? 
Can you think of any buildings or structures you have seen that may be based on this fold?

➜ NOTE
There are other videos that you can watch on Vimeo that will give examples of more projects in the book. Search for "Paul Jackson Folding Techniques" or "Paul Jackson Completes Pleats."
